title: Smallholding livestock calendar
subtitle: A seasonal reference for the tasks that repeat across a livestock-keeping year.
excerpt: Most livestock management follows a rhythm that repeats year on year. Knowing what is coming up a month ahead means you can prepare equipment, plan vet visits, and order supplies before the moment of need arrives.
sourceNotes: NADIS, seasonal livestock management calendar. AHDB, yearly planning guides for beef, sheep, and pigs. SAC Consulting, farm management planning notes.

content.text: The calendar below covers sheep, pigs, and poultry for a typical UK holding. Exact timing varies by region, breed, and local conditions. Treat this as a framework rather than a fixed schedule; a hill farm in the north of England lambs 6 weeks later than a lowland farm in the south.

Sheep: shearing (June/July), fly prevention treatments, foot bathing for foot rot, weaning of spring lambs at 12 to 16 weeks. Pigs: wallow provision and shade management. Poultry: monitor red mite in warm weather, keep water cool and clean, watch for broody behaviour.
Sheep: condition score ewes and body condition target for tupping, begin

two weeks before the ram goes in (October for most breeds), wormer check at housing. Store hay buying and stacking. Pigs: move to winter housing, build up deep litter beds. Poultry: autumn moult begins, egg production falls; expect this and do not mistake moult for illness.
Sheep: supplementary hay and concentrate feeding ramps up as pregnancy progresses, clostridial vaccination of ewes four to six weeks before lambing.

boosters for the whole flock. Prepare lambing area and kit. Pigs: check bedding daily, break ice on troughs, monitor condition. Poultry: reduce flock stress during short days, provide additional light if egg production is a priority.

Sheep: lambing (March/April for most breeds), ear tagging and castrating lambs within the first week. Post-lambing ewe condition check and worming as appropriate. Turn out onto spring grass when available. Pigs: move outdoors as conditions allow, set up paddock rotation, piglet castration and ear notching if your system uses these. Poultry: egg production rises with day length, broodies become common; manage or encourage depending on your plan.
